changes: Injected MainViewModel using viewModels() delegate with @AndroidEntryPoint annotation
Added comprehensive documentation explaining viewModels() delegate behavior and Hilt integration
ViewModel property remains unused in onCreate() - placeholder for future UI composition
Binary Gradle build cache file updated
file_insights.note: Typically auto-generated and not intended for version control

patterns_discovered.description: @AndroidEntryPoint annotation enables automatic field injection at Activity level
patterns_discovered.implementation: Used on MainActivity class for Hilt integration

patterns_discovered.description: Modern androidx pattern using by viewModels() for automatic ViewModel creation and lifecycle management
patterns_discovered.implementation: private val viewModel: MainViewModel by viewModels()
patterns_discovered.benefit: Automatic retention across configuration changes, memory-efficient

patterns_discovered.description: Multi-line KDoc comments explaining injected dependencies and their behavior

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Gradle binary file in version control

gotchas_discovered.description: ViewModel is injected but not actually used in onCreate() method - only placeholder comment exists
gotchas_discovered.impact: Dead code pattern - ViewModel should be referenced when UI composition is implemented

gotchas_discovered.description: Implementation assumes MainViewModel exists and is annotated with @HiltViewModel
gotchas_discovered.impact: Runtime error if MainViewModel is not properly configured as Hilt-injectable

approach_outcome.description: Successfully integrated Hilt-based ViewModel injection into MainActivity
key_achievements: Proper use of viewModels() delegate for lifecycle-aware ViewModel management
Correct @AndroidEntryPoint annotation placement for Activity-level Hilt integration
Clear documentation of dependency injection pattern
Follows Android Architecture Components best practices
approach_outcome.completeness: Architectural setup complete; UI integration pending
